name: Refrigerator No-Cheese Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the graham crackers in a plastic bag and tie the top. Crush with a rolling pin or jar to make fine crumbs.
2. Melt the margarine in the bottom of an 8- or 9-inch round cake pan. Spread evenly over the bottom of the pan.
3. Sprinkle the crumbs evenly on the bottom only. Press gently. Chill in the refrigerator.
4. Soak gelatin in cold water. Heat over boiling water to dissolve the gelatin.
5. Combine yogurt and first measure of sweetener (1 tbsp sugar substitute) in a large bowl. Beat at moderate speed with rotary beaters, adding dissolved gelatin gradually.
6. Chill until it is the consistency of unbitten egg whites.
7. In another large bowl, combine iced water, lemon rind, egg white, and dry milk powder. Beat with rotary beaters until soft peaks form.
8. Add lemon juice, vanilla, and second measure of sweetener (1 tb sugar substitute). Beat at high speed until stiff.
9. Fold the stiff mixture into the partially set yogurt. Blend very well.
10. Spoon the mixture on top of the crumbs in the pan. Chill for 4 hours or longer until set.
11. To unmold, loosen around the edge of the mold with a thin spatula right down to the bottom of the pan.
12. Place a larger plate upside down on top of the mold. Turn the plate and pan over. Cover the top of the pan with a hot cloth that has been run under hot water and then rung out for a few seconds.
13. Remove the cloth and lift the pan from the mold. When ready to serve, garnish with cherries or strawberries and cut into six equal slices.
6 Servings 1 Serving: 1/6 Cake
Nutritive values per serving: 13 gm. carbohydrates; 8 gm. protein; 2 gm. fat; 104 calories; 0.3 gm. fiber; 124 mg. sodium; 6 mg. cholesterol
Food Exchange per serving: 1 Low-Fat Milk Exchange
Low-sodium diets: This recipe is suitable.
